body: Bank Negara Indonesia (BNI) reported a net profit of IDR 19.81 trillion in November 2024, a 4.04% increase from the same period last year, driven by a 10.96% growth in lending to IDR 739.53 trillion and a 9.83% increase in total assets to IDR 1,072.63 trillion. Despite a 23.30% increase in interest expense, BNI's third-party funds grew 6.96% to IDR 783.78 trillion, with low-cost fund instruments and current accounts being key contributors to this growth.
title: Bank Negara Indonesia reports 4.04% increase in net profit to IDR 19.81 trillion in November 2024.

body: PT Golden Energy Mines Tbk (GEMS) has increased the authorized capital of its subsidiary, PT Trisula Kencana Sakti (TKS), to 900,000 shares with a nominal value of IDR450 billion, up from IDR400 billion previously. The capital increase, funded by GEMS' internal cash, aims to strengthen TKS' financial position, with GEMS holding a 99.99% effective ownership percentage and voting rights in TKS.
title: PT Golden Energy Mines increases capital of subsidiary PT Trisula Kencana Sakti to strengthen financial position.

body: Shikoku Electric Power will acquire 25% of PT Hero Global Investment Tbk (HGII)'s shares, equivalent to 1.62 billion shares, from its controlling shareholders after the company's initial public offering (IPO) on January 3-7, 2025, for a potential fresh funding of IDR 299 billion. After the acquisition, the controlling shareholders, Rudy Chandra, Robert Njo, and Hendrianto Thamrin, will retain a 55% stake in HGII, down from 80% currently.
title: Shikoku Electric Power to acquire 25% stake in PT Hero Global Investment after its initial public offering.

body: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM) will issue shelf registration bonds in Phase III, targeting to raise IDR 303.2 billion, as part of its IDR 1.1 trillion bond program, with coupons ranging from 7.70% to 8.80%. The proceeds will be allocated towards working capital, with 70% for warehousing needs and 30% for margin financing, with the public offering period set for January 9-10, 2025, and listing on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on January 16, 2025.
title: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia to Issue IDR 303.2 Billion Bonds in Phase III of Shelf Registration Program

body: PT Campina Ice Cream Industry TBK (CAMP) reported a significant decline in net profit to IDR 59 billion in the first nine months of 2024, down from IDR 112 billion in the same period last year, due to increased competition from new players like Mixue. Despite this, the company remains optimistic about the Indonesian ice cream market's potential, citing low per-capita consumption and plans to launch more affordable products to offset competition and drive growth in 2025.
title: Campina Ice Cream Industry Addresses Slowing Profit Amid Rising Competition in Indonesian Market

body: PT Samcro Hyosung Adilestari Tbk (ACRO) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 7.59 billion, with a dividend payout ratio of 94.88%, following a decision by the board of directors, with a dividend value per share of around IDR 2.19. The company's share price has increased 34.57% to IDR 109 per share, making it the third top gainer, after posting a net profit of IDR 8 billion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Samcro Hyosung Adilestari to Distribute IDR 7.59 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi Tbk (OBAT) is set to raise IDR 59.5 billion in its initial public offering (IPO) by selling 170 million shares at IDR 330-350 per share, exceeding its total assets of IDR 52.08 billion as of June 30, 2024. The IPO proceeds will be used to purchase raw materials, increase production capacity, and develop marketing, with the company operating in the business fields of herbal makloon, cosmetics, and functional botanical drinks.
title: PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi to Raise IDR 59.5 Billion in IPO with 170 Million Shares Offered.

body: PT Techno9 Indonesia Tbk (T9) will be acquired by Poh Group Pte Ltd, which will take over 70% of the company's shares from majority shareholders, making Poh Group the controlling shareholder. The acquisition, part of Poh Group's business development expansion strategy, will trigger a mandatory tender offer, and T9's share price has already risen 10% to IDR 99 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 213.54 billion.
title: PT Techno9 Indonesia to be Acquired by Poh Group Pte Ltd in Deal Worth 70% of Shares

body: PT Estika Tata Tiara TBK (ESTI) achieved significant growth through strategic expansions, including adding distribution facilities in key locations, optimizing its feedlot capacity, and planning to import 12,000 head of live cattle and frozen meat by 2025. The company also acquired three large distributor companies, including PT Fajar Jaya Anugerah, PT Sinar Wijaya Utama, and PT Sukses International Anugerah Pratama, to meet increasing market demand and support consumer needs for high-quality products.
title: PT Estika Tata Tiara TBK Sees Significant Growth Through Distribution Expansion and Product Innovation

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index fell 4.65% to 7,324.79 last week, driven by low liquidity and a 17.71% decline in average volume of shares traded, with foreign investors recording a net sell of IDR 4.09 trillion. PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK (none) led the gainers with a 7.92% increase, while PT Bank Mandiri TBK (BMRI) was the top loser with a 5.81% decline, as all sectoral indices showed declines, with the basic materials sector falling the most at 8.21%.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Falls 4.65% Amid Low Liquidity and Foreign Net Sell.

body: The Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index (JCI) closed 0.39% higher at 6,983.87, with a transaction value of IDR 12.48 trillion (USD 767 million) and a trading volume of 19.51 billion shares. Despite foreign investors selling IDR 417.99 billion (USD 25.7 million) worth of shares, the energy, non-cyclical consumer, healthcare, finance, technology, and infrastructure sectors drove the JCI's performance, with PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK (BARE) shares rising 4.25% and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK (BBRI) shares falling 0.73%.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Closes Higher After Six-Day Decline, Up 0.39% to 6,983.87.

body: Wilo (no ticker symbol), a German company specializing in modern pump technology, has partnered with the Indonesian Defense University to implement advanced pump systems across various sectors, including defense, with a focus on enhancing energy efficiency and environmental sustainability. The partnership includes a donation of modern pumps for student research purposes, aiming to enhance students' capabilities in addressing modern technological challenges and potentially leading to further collaboration opportunities between the university and industry partners.
title: Indonesian Defense University partners with German company Wilo to implement advanced pump systems and enhance student capabilities.
